title: HMW DNA extraction for Long Read Sequencing using CTAB
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"705kg","text":"High Molecular Weight DNA extraction protocol for Long Read sequencing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79e2r","text":"Extraction is performed from flash frozen leaves stored at -80°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2dnqq","text":"The protocol is adapted for an extraction of 1g of leaves.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5a97b","text":"This protocol is based on the protocol provided by Oxford Nanopore Technologies, Oxford, UK (ONT), \"High molecular weight gDNA extraction from plant leaves\" provided by the ONT community in March 2019, with slighly modifications. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1uf1c","text":"This protocol involves a conventional CTAB extraction followed by purification using commercial Qiagen Genomic tips (QIAGEN, MD, USA). DNA fragment size selection is performed using Short Read Eliminator (Circulomics, MD, USA).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tfq6","text":"This protocol is particularly adapted for plant leaves, but also works with many other organisms (microalgae, insects...).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"es1lv","text":"Use only wide bore tips.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7aa2j","text":"Work in a chemical hood when using 2-mercaptoethanol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fih7","text":"Allow the DNA to resuspend for at least of 24 hours before proceeding with QC.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9e5cs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Extraction Buffer : Prepare 20 mL of extraction buffer (per 1g of leaves) in a 50 mL tube Reagents Final concentration 1 Tris-HCl ph8 100 mM 2 EDTA 20 mM 3 CTAB 2% 4 NaCl 1.4. M 5 PEG 8000 1 % 6 H2O QSP 20 ml 7

Add 50 µl of 2-Mercaptoethanol to 20 ml of extraction buffer and preheat to 65 °C (approx. 15 min). 20 mL, Extraction buffer 50 µL 2-mercaptoethanol Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #M6250 65 °C
Put a mortar in ice. Cool the mortar and pestle with liquid nitrogen until the bubbling stops. Liquid nitrogen Contributed by users
Grind 1g of frozen sample to a fine powder (approx. 2 min), without adding liquid nitrogen. 1 g 00:02:00 Before grinding After grinding
Transfer the powder to the pre-warmed extraction buffer
Add 40 µl of RNase A (100mg/ml) and vortex the tube 5 sec. 40 µL RNase A Qiagen Catalog #19101 00:00:05, Vortex
Incubate 1h at 65°C with intermittent agitation (300 rpm every 10 min) 01:00:00, 65°C Eppendorf ThermoMixer C pipette.com Catalog #2231000667
Let the tube cool for 5 min at RT 00:05:00, RT
Add 1 volume (20ml) of chloroform and vortex 2x5sec. 20 mL Chloroform Contributed by users
Centrifuge with low acceleration and deceleration 5500 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes, Acc/Dec : 6/3 Before centrifugation After centrifugation
Gently transfer the aqueous phase to a new 50 ml tube.
Add 0.7 volume of isopropanol and mix gently by inversion 10 times Isopropanol Contributed by users
Put the tube at -80°C for 15 minutes. 00:15:00, -80°C

If a DNA medusa appears after this step, recover the medusa using a Pasteur pipette (without breaking the tip of the pipette). Wash the medusa in 3 successive baths of 70% ethanol then resuspend the medusa in 100µl of 1X TE. Incubate for 1 hour at 55°C then store the tube at 4°C before quality control. Continue the protocol with the rest of the tube (without the medusa). If no DNA medusa is visible continue the protocol.
Centrifuge the tube with low acceleration and deceleration 5500 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes, Acc/Dec : 6/3
Carrefully remove the supernatant without resuspending the pellet. Remove the remaining liquid by turning the tube upside down on a paper towel (make sure the pellet does not come off).
Gently resuspend with the pipette the DNA pellet with 9.5 ml of G2 buffer (QIAGEN Genomic-tip 100/G). Buffer G2 Qiagen Catalog #1014636
Incubate 15 min at 50°C . The sample can be stored overnight at 4°C at this stage. 00:15:00, 50°C
Equilibrate a QIAGEN Genomic-tip 100/G column with 4 ml of QBT buffer . Genomic tip 100G Qiagen Catalog #10223 Buffer QBT Qiagen Catalog #19054
Allow all the QBT buffer to drain by gravity flow into a 50 ml tube.
Add the sample in G2 buffer (9.5ml) on the column and let it to enter the resin by gravity flow.
Wash the column twice with 7.5 ml of QC buffer. Buffer QC Qiagen Catalog #19055
Put the column on a 15 ml tube.
Elute the DNA with 5 ml of QF buffer preheated to 55°C. Buffer QF Qiagen Catalog #19056
Preheat 5 ml of QF buffer to 55°C. Buffer QF Qiagen Catalog #19056
Add 0.7 volume of isopropanol to the eluate. Mix gently by inverting 20 times. Isopropanol Contributed by users
Incubate 15 minutes at RT. 00:15:00, RT
Centrifuge with low acceleration and deceleration 5500 x g, 4 °C, 30 minutes, Acc/Dec : 6/3
Carefully discard the supernatant, gently resuspend the pellet with 1 ml of cold 70% ethanol. 70% Ethanol Contributed by users
Transfer the resuspended DNA to a 1.5 ml DNA LoBind tube. DNA LoBind Tube 1.5ml Eppendorf Catalog #022431021
Centrifuge with low acceleration and deceleration 5000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes, Acc/Dec : 6/3
Carefully discard the supernatant. Air dry the pellet at RT for about 10 minutes. 00:10:00, RT
Resuspend the pellet with 50-100 µl of 1X TE buffer. Buffer TE 1x Contributed by users
Allow the DNA to resuspend for 2 hours at 55°C or ON at RT. 02:00:00, 55°C or Overnight, RT
Store the DNA at 4°C.

Quantify your sample with a Qubit DNA HS assay. Check the purity of the sample with a Nanodrop (measurements of 260/280 and 260/230 absorbance ratios). Estimate the molecular weight of the sample with a Tapestation and/or a Femto pulse and/or a Pippin Pulse.
QC results obtained on different species.
Depending on the DNA concentrations and DNA length profiles, deplete short DNA molecules using SRE size selection kits (SRE XS, SRE or SRE XL kits).
creator: Benoît Vacherie
